ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Hewlett, 415.515.7860, plaintiff in propria persona.
James Braden, Esq., 415.398.6865, defendant in propria persona.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JAMES BRADEN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff cannot establish causation or damages as a matter of law. The court takes judicial notice of the federal filings. The order dismissing appeal issued by Judge Breyer expressly found that there was no basis to appeal. The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals affirmed that finding and similarly found that plaintiff's appeal was frivolous. "[A] client claiming that his [her] attorney was negligent in connection with litigation has the burden of proving that damages resulted, this burden involving, usually, the difficult task of demonstrating that, but for the negligence complained of, the client would have been successful in the prosecution or defense of the action in question." Sukoff v. Lemkin (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 740, 744. In this case, even if defendant owed a duty to file an appeal, there was nothing he could have done to lead to a better outcome for plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Carol Karen #8189.
